title: Doc changes to fix alignment on most of the docs
body:
Went through the docs and fixed the alignment so they
all match up and look readable when doing 'man podman [command]"

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: Consistency is always a good thing. Should we be going with no-whitespace instead of the whitespace line, though?

@mheon the whitespace line makes it more readable in my opinion. But whatever majority prefers :)
I just wanted the alignment to be consistent.

Oh also, I noticed sometimes without the whitespace and with it tabbed it would generate weirdly, but could just be my shell. Not sure if the type of shell you use makes a difference in how the docs are rendered.
@umohnani8 Could you look at how these pages render in markdown on github, to make sure that they look good both as man pages in the terminal as well as man pages on github.

LGTM, Gold star for @umohnani8 .  @rhatdan I popped a few up by hitting the view button on three or four of the man pages and they looked good to me.

@wking any opinions?
@rhatdan double checked on github, the formatting looks good!

:pushpin: Commit 0ccf3a2 has been approved by `rhatdan`
